reviews.review_title: Fun  and unique game but abounded
reviews.review_description:
Edit: sadly after what i see... the game is forgotten still good but storyline is what i see forgotten...and in end it is worth only at very low price because as much as visually it look nice and gameplay is unique story is cut short and so sadly i leaving low ratings 



I'll start with what you need to be aware of: the hand controls can get infuriating . But the hand stuff it's more of lighting and technology limitations so just breathe and relax  and the problems that i had was very low and the dev were quick to respond and help now for the other stuff it's gorgeous game with a fantastic music and interesting and unique gameplay  and I would say I enjoyed it very much and I think it's worth it so 5/5 would recommend to buy! And i can't wait to see more of them!

reviews.review_title: Explore. Wonder. Nice secret areas. Good
reviews.review_description: Be patient and use controllers and you can get the hang of using items and powers. Not that many puzzles and the progression is not organised in a very coherent way but exploration is rewarded usually. Getting powers by playing your instrument(ocarina of time style) is cool but having to keep doing it because they are timed is a bit annoying. The temple and the end boss area have a great sense of scale are great to explore.  Get it on sale.

Hand tracking! Do I need to say more? Everyone who has tried it knows how hard it is to make it work. I switched to controllers, which improved things, but spending so much time trying to grab objects isn’t fun for me. The game also kicked me out a few times. Maybe it's just me, but I didn't find the puzzles very intuitive; most of the time I didn’t know what to do, even outside the puzzles. It’s a shame because the art and music are amazing. I also don’t understand what half of the potions do! Why didn't they just make a regular controller game with good grabbing mechanics? I don't get why they chose this direction.

I bought this game for about $2 and I can see why it’s that cheap.

The hand tracking is horrible. Makes the game completely unplayable and not very fun.

Lore and setting seems interesting. I would love to play it if it wasn’t for these awful controls.
This is the only game that made me sick while riding the animal. I cannot progress further into the game.

I had played Ionia, the previous entry which was polished but short, so I was expecting a similar experience with Eolia.  I was impressed with how much bigger this game felt.  Not only does it have lengthy story campaign, but there is a great mix of large outdoor spaces and detailed interior environments.  The gameplay is focused on puzzles, platforming, and finding collectibles, but the boss level late in the game was a nice challenging addition.  I tried the hand tracking controls for the first hour which work OK but not perfectly, so I switched back to controllers after that.  The story is music-focused and may not be everyone's cup of tea, but it's encouraging to see that there are developers willing to make a unique single player VR adventure that isn't just another wave shooter or rouge-like.

The controls/UI are super unintuitive and inflexible, and I'm not even using hand controls. Take the inventory for example. Any time your palm is up, the menu activates, which is really annoying. Then to access your storage pouch, you have to grab it and put it horizontal in the air -- wrong angle and it'll just fail. Then to store objects, you can't just drop it at the slot -- you have to drop it at a (unintuitive) icon. And to retrieve it, you can't just grab it out like in every other game -- you have to poke it with your finger, which will select it, then you can grab it (from another location). There are many things that could've been done better, such as when your hand is over some interactable object, it should be highlighted. Locomotion is also very clunky. Acceleration is very slow (which doesn't actually make it less nauseating), and it hinders my ability to do running jumps. Collision physics is also not good. There are many more examples. Now, the good parts -- scenes/graphics, music/sounds/voice (though not nearly as good as Ionia), and maybe story. If you are *really* patient or somehow think exactly like the designers do, maybe this is worth a try. Also, riding the animal was extremely nauseating.

The graphics and music are great, but the hand tracking is difficult to get working smoothly. Even in the Tutorial the walking movement wasn’t readily recognized, and the turning was so glitching that I felt dizzy and nauseated. So I stopped playing, and will let one of my older grandchildren give it a try. I may try it with the controllers in the future to see if I have better luck.

If you experience motion sickness in VR, be warned there are no comfort settings in this game! Those snap turns and fast movements will have you puking in no time! I couldn’t go past the first minute of this game! 🤢
I was a bit scared of at first when reviews mentioned the bad hand tracking. But the game is great both ways. There has been an updated. 04/05/24

reviews.review_title: Seems cool, but makes me nauseous
reviews.review_description: It seems really cool, but unfortunately I couldn't get past the first driving segment because it made me sick.

This game is fun and has a great story. But what really is impressive is how well they use hand track they were able to put in full locomotion with turning and jumping while utilizing hand tracking which is pretty amazing after about five or 10 minutes of getting used in the controls I had no problem finding the rest of the game with hand tracking And the good thing is if you don’t like the hand tracking , you can always use your controllers. I would recommend this game to anyone looking for something unique

Elioa sounds like a great game but there are some bugs.The game has a part where there are these things called dust devils and that part  of the game is impossible to win. You ride these things (that I forgot the name of) and to slow them down you pull back and the game decides to glitch your position back and it will slow you down or stop you, the allowing the dust devils to get you. 

Elioa has a great concept to it but the glitches make the game impossible to win but if the developer or developers fixed that, it would make the game more playable

developer_response.body:
We encourage you to join our Discord community, where you can receive additional support and get the opportunity to try out updates before they are released to the public. Our team and other players would be happy to assist you in resolving any technical issues you may encounter while playing the game.

We are constantly working to improve Eolia, and we appreciate your feedback on the game's concept. Our developers are aware of the Lhargo ride and dust devil issues for some users you have encountered and are actively working to fix them to make the game more enjoyable for everyone.

Best regards,
The ROTU Team

reviews.review_description:
I loved Ionia so I had high hopes for this. The environment looks gorgeous and a music-based puzzle game sounds right up my alley.

That's the end of the good. Hand tracking is still shaky (I lost my left hand before finishing the tutorial. And, to be clear, I could see my hand but could no longer bring up the movement glyph so was stuck in place). Luckily controllers are allowed, but then I started the game and can't even make it past the dust devils. Can't find any way to change the difficulty setting. Frustrating. Refund.

developer_response.body:
Hi MisssElaineous, We appreciate your interest in Ionia, I remember your review! We understand that you are facing difficulties with hand tracking and game difficulty, and we want to assist you in resolving these problems.

We kindly invite you to join our Discord community, https://discord.com/invite/gvM6esfJ93, where you can receive additional support and get the opportunity to try out updates before they are released to the public. Our team and fellow players are always eager to help in any way we can.

At ROTU, we take great pride in creating games that provide an enjoyable and immersive experience. We assure you that we are working to fix them to enhance the overall gameplay. The Dust Devil issue is fixed and ready for the next update.

reviews.review_description: The first and second part where you have to escape the sand devil is the hardest thing to do in the game, its too hard for the start of the game I was excited to get it but then I played it for 15 minutes and couldn't even play it I don't recommend buying this game

The puzzles and story are good but the game is quite glitchy. I would often complete a puzzle but the game would not initiate the next sequence. I would have to exit the game and redo the puzzle (the same way), or exit the area and reenter, then it would work. It also doesn't save your progress properly, I think I had to do about 30 percent of the game twice because it wouldn't save. A handful of times the game just crashed and brought me back to the oculus menu. The hand tracking is cool but it doesn't work reliably. I switched to the controller's and it was fine.

reviews.review_title: How to use Hand Tracking properly
reviews.review_description: Controllers send wireless info to the headset… but your bare hands cannot.  Remember that the cameras system on your headset is now the controller.  If the headset can’t see your fingers well, then neither can the game.  When making an OK sign to move around for example - aim the O at your face so your thumbs don’t block your index fingers.  The hardware will be much less glitchy that way - and the game receives better data.  Given you can see your hands in game, the more intentionally you can see your own gestures the better hand tracking hardware performs!  Explore the Journal in game for this any many other great tips…

reviews.review_title: Great use of music and hand tracking
reviews.review_description: Innovative and immersive gameplay especially the way music figures both as a gameplay element and as dramatic underscore.

Step one get used to using hand-tracking, some things good other hard to learn. Step two pick up controllers whenever you want. The game is beautiful and supports non profits. Please support games that are not zombie shooters.

reviews.review_title: Great game with a great story
reviews.review_description: This game is amazing the story and the meaning behind it is wow, it all has the music theme in the back and going back to a imaginary time long ago is great and very well developed. The hand controls are amazing and the fact that you can play a game with your hands is new technology. Overall amazing game
